rule.hazard: non-compliance with Union harmonisation legislation
conditions: product contains an AI system subject to this Regulation and Union harmonisation legislation listed in Section A of Annex I
requirements: Providers shall ensure that their product is fully compliant with all applicable requirements under the Union harmonisation legislation listed in Section A of Annex I.

rule.hazard: harm to safety
requirements: org shall establish a risk management system in relation to high-risk AI systems.

rule.hazard: harm to safety due to lifecycle gaps
conditions: "throughout the entire lifecycle of a high-risk AI system"
requirements: org shall plan and run the risk management system as a continuous iterative process throughout the entire lifecycle of a high-risk AI system.

rule.hazard: outdated or ineffective risk controls
conditions: requires "regular systematic review and updating"
requirements: org shall conduct regular systematic review and updating of the risk management system.

rule.hazard: harm to safety from intended use
conditions: "when the high-risk AI system is used in accordance with its intended purpose"
requirements: org shall identify and analyse the known and reasonably foreseeable risks that the high-risk AI system can pose to safety when the system is used in accordance with its intended purpose.

rule.hazard: harm to safety from foreseeable misuse
conditions: "when the high-risk AI system is used in accordance with its intended purpose, and under conditions of reasonably foreseeable misuse"
requirements: org shall estimate and evaluate the risks that may emerge under intended use and under conditions of reasonably foreseeable misuse.

rule.hazard: unmitigated safety risks
conditions: "to address the risks identified pursuant to point (a)"
requirements: org shall adopt appropriate and targeted risk management measures designed to address the risks identified pursuant to point (a).

rule.hazard: increased residual risk due to unconsidered interactions between requirements
conditions: measures referred to in paragraph 2, point (d)
combined application of the requirements set out in this Section
requirements: developer shall ensure that risk management measures give due consideration to the effects and possible interaction resulting from the combined application of the requirements set out in this Section in order to minimise risks while achieving an appropriate balance in implementing the measures.

rule.hazard: unacceptable residual risk of the high-risk AI system
conditions: measures referred to in paragraph 2, point (d)
requirements: developer shall ensure that the relevant residual risk associated with each hazard, as well as the overall residual risk of the high-risk AI system, is judged to be acceptable.

rule.hazard: identified risks not eliminated or reduced through design and development
conditions: in identifying the most appropriate risk management measures
pursuant to paragraph 2
requirements: developer shall eliminate or reduce risks identified and evaluated pursuant to paragraph 2 as far as technically feasible through adequate design and development of the high-risk AI system.

rule.hazard: unmitigated residual risks that cannot be eliminated
conditions: in identifying the most appropriate risk management measures
for risks that cannot be eliminated
requirements: developer shall implement adequate mitigation and control measures addressing risks that cannot be eliminated.

rule.hazard: deployers lacking required information or training
conditions: in identifying the most appropriate risk management measures
provision of information required pursuant to Article 13
requirements: developer shall provide the information required pursuant to Article 13 to deployers and shall provide training to deployers where appropriate.

rule.hazard: insufficiently identified or targeted risk management measures
requirements: Developer shall test high-risk AI systems for the purpose of identifying the most appropriate and targeted risk management measures.

rule.hazard: inconsistent performance for intended purpose
requirements: Developer shall test high-risk AI systems to verify they perform consistently for their intended purpose.

rule.hazard: non-compliance with the requirements set out in this Section
requirements: Developer shall test high-risk AI systems to verify they are in compliance with the requirements set out in this Section.

rule.hazard: deployment of untested high-risk AI systems
conditions: prior to being placed on the market or put into service
requirements: provider shall perform testing of high-risk AI systems prior to their being placed on the market or put into service.

rule.hazard: deployment of high-risk AI systems that do not meet defined performance thresholds
requirements: provider shall carry out testing against prior defined metrics and probabilistic thresholds that are appropriate to the intended purpose of the high-risk AI system.

rule.hazard: adverse impact on persons under the age of 18
conditions: when implementing the risk management system as provided for in paragraphs 1 to 7
requirements: provider shall give consideration to whether, in view of its intended purpose, the high-risk AI system is likely to have an adverse impact on persons under the age of 18.

rule.hazard: inaccurate model behavior due to low-quality training data
conditions: whenever training, validation and testing data sets are used
requirements: The developer shall develop high-risk AI systems that make use of techniques involving the training of AI models on the basis of training, validation and testing data sets that meet the quality criteria referred to in paragraphs 2 to 5.

rule.hazard: inadequate governance causing unsafe or non-compliant outcomes
conditions: for training, validation and testing data sets
for the intended purpose of the high-risk AI system
requirements: The developer shall subject training, validation and testing data sets to data governance and management practices appropriate for the intended purpose of the high-risk AI system.

rule.hazard: bias causing harm to health or discrimination
conditions: for training, validation and testing data sets
"in view of possible biases that are likely to affect the health and safety of persons, have a negative impact on fundamental rights or lead to discrimination prohibited under Union law"
requirements: The developer shall examine training, validation and testing data sets in view of possible biases that are likely to affect the health and safety of persons, have a negative impact on fundamental rights or lead to discrimination prohibited under Union law.

rule.hazard: undetected biases causing discrimination or harm
conditions: for training, validation and testing data sets
where biases are identified according to point (f)
requirements: The developer shall implement appropriate measures to detect, prevent and mitigate possible biases identified according to point (f).

rule.hazard: use of personal data contrary to original purpose causing privacy violations
conditions: for training, validation and testing data sets
in the case of personal data
requirements: The developer shall ensure data governance practices address data collection processes and the origin of data and, in the case of personal data, the original purpose of the data collection.

rule.hazard: data gaps causing non-compliance with the Regulation
conditions: for training, validation and testing data sets
that prevent compliance with this Regulation
requirements: The developer shall identify relevant data gaps or shortcomings that prevent compliance with this Regulation and specify how those gaps and shortcomings can be addressed.

rule.hazard: irrelevant training data leading to inappropriate system behavior
conditions: in view of the intended purpose
requirements: developer shall ensure that training, validation and testing data sets are relevant to the intended purpose.

rule.hazard: biased model outcomes due to unrepresentative data
conditions: in view of the intended purpose
requirements: developer shall ensure that training, validation and testing data sets are sufficiently representative in view of the intended purpose.

rule.hazard: errors or incompleteness in data causing incorrect model behavior
conditions: to the best extent possible
in view of the intended purpose
requirements: developer shall ensure that training, validation and testing data sets are, to the best extent possible, free of errors and complete in view of the intended purpose.

rule.hazard: statistical bias affecting persons or groups of persons
conditions: including, where applicable, as regards the persons or groups of persons in relation to whom the high-risk AI system is intended to be used
requirements: developer shall ensure that training, validation and testing data sets have appropriate statistical properties.

rule.hazard: unauthorized access or misuse of special categories of personal data
conditions: processing is strictly necessary for ensuring bias detection and correction in relation to high-risk AI systems in accordance with paragraph (2), points (f) and (g) of this Article
if relating to development of high-risk AI systems not using techniques involving the training of AI models, paragraphs 2 to 5 apply only to testing data sets
requirements: provider shall verify and document that bias detection and correction cannot be effectively fulfilled by processing other data, including synthetic or anonymised data.

rule.hazard: unauthorized access or misuse of special categories of personal data
conditions: processing is strictly necessary for ensuring bias detection and correction in relation to high-risk AI systems in accordance with paragraph (2), points (f) and (g) of this Article
if relating to development of high-risk AI systems not using techniques involving the training of AI models, paragraphs 2 to 5 apply only to testing data sets
requirements: provider shall ensure that the special categories of personal data are subject to technical limitations on re-use and that state-of-the-art security and privacy-preserving measures, including pseudonymisation, are implemented.

rule.hazard: unauthorized access or misuse of special categories of personal data
conditions: processing is strictly necessary for ensuring bias detection and correction in relation to high-risk AI systems in accordance with paragraph (2), points (f) and (g) of this Article
if relating to development of high-risk AI systems not using techniques involving the training of AI models, paragraphs 2 to 5 apply only to testing data sets
requirements: provider shall implement measures to ensure that the special categories of personal data processed are secured and protected with suitable safeguards, including strict controls and documentation of access, and that only authorised persons with appropriate confidentiality obligations have access to those personal data to avoid misuse.

rule.hazard: unauthorized disclosure of special categories of personal data
conditions: processing is strictly necessary for ensuring bias detection and correction in relation to high-risk AI systems in accordance with paragraph (2), points (f) and (g) of this Article
if relating to development of high-risk AI systems not using techniques involving the training of AI models, paragraphs 2 to 5 apply only to testing data sets
requirements: provider shall not transmit, transfer or otherwise allow access to the special categories of personal data by other parties.

rule.hazard: retention of sensitive personal data beyond its necessary use
conditions: processing is strictly necessary for ensuring bias detection and correction in relation to high-risk AI systems in accordance with paragraph (2), points (f) and (g) of this Article
if relating to development of high-risk AI systems not using techniques involving the training of AI models, paragraphs 2 to 5 apply only to testing data sets
requirements: provider shall delete the special categories of personal data once the bias has been corrected or the personal data has reached the end of its retention period, whichever comes first.

rule.hazard: lack of documented necessity for processing special categories of personal data
conditions: processing is strictly necessary for ensuring bias detection and correction in relation to high-risk AI systems in accordance with paragraph (2), points (f) and (g) of this Article
if relating to development of high-risk AI systems not using techniques involving the training of AI models, paragraphs 2 to 5 apply only to testing data sets
requirements: provider shall ensure that the records of processing activities pursuant to Regulations (EU) 2016/679 and (EU) 2018/1725 and Directive (EU) 2016/680 include the reasons why the processing of special categories of personal data was strictly necessary to detect and correct biases and why that objective could not be achieved by processing other data.
evidence: records of processing activities (reasons why processing was strictly necessary; why other data insufficient)

rule.hazard: inability for authorities to assess compliance
conditions: before the system is placed on the market or put into service
requirements: The org shall draw up the technical documentation of the high-risk AI system before the system is placed on the market or put into service.

rule.hazard: inability for authorities to assess compliance
requirements: The org shall keep the technical documentation up-to-date.

rule.hazard: failure to demonstrate compliance with regulatory requirements
requirements: The org shall prepare the technical documentation so that it demonstrates that the high-risk AI system complies with the requirements set out in this Section.

rule.hazard: inability for authorities to assess compliance
requirements: The org shall prepare the technical documentation to provide national competent authorities and notified bodies with the necessary information in a clear and comprehensive form to assess the compliance of the AI system with those requirements.

rule.hazard: incomplete technical documentation relative to regulatory requirements
conditions: Annex IV
requirements: The org shall ensure the technical documentation contains, at a minimum, the elements set out in Annex IV.

rule.hazard: non-compliance with Annex IV technical documentation
conditions: opts to provide the information required in Annex IV in a simplified manner
requirements: org shall use the simplified technical documentation form referred to in this paragraph when it opts to provide the information required in Annex IV in a simplified manner.

rule.hazard: insufficient technical documentation
conditions: high-risk AI system related to a product covered by the Union harmonisation legislation listed in Section A of Annex I
placed on the market or put into service
requirements: Draw up a single set of technical documentation.

rule.hazard: missing required information from paragraph 1
conditions: high-risk AI system related to a product covered by the Union harmonisation legislation listed in Section A of Annex I
placed on the market or put into service
requirements: Ensure the technical documentation contains all the information set out in paragraph 1.

rule.hazard: non-compliance with applicable legal acts due to missing information
conditions: high-risk AI system related to a product covered by the Union harmonisation legislation listed in Section A of Annex I
placed on the market or put into service
requirements: Ensure the technical documentation contains the information required under those legal acts.

rule.hazard: inability to trace system events over its lifetime
requirements: developer shall implement technical capability to automatically record events (logs) over the lifetime of the high-risk AI system.

rule.hazard: undetected situations that cause the system to present a risk or undergo substantial modification
requirements: developer shall enable logging capabilities to record events relevant for identifying situations that may result in the high-risk AI system presenting a risk or in a substantial modification.

rule.hazard: inability to facilitate post-market monitoring
requirements: developer shall enable logging capabilities to record events relevant for facilitating the post-market monitoring referred to in Article 72.

rule.hazard: inability to monitor the operation of high-risk AI systems
requirements: developer shall enable logging capabilities to record events relevant for monitoring the operation of high-risk AI systems referred to in Article 26(5).

rule.hazard: inability to reconstruct usage timelines
conditions: Annex III point 1(a) system
requirements: developer shall provide logging that records the period of each use (start date and time and end date and time of each use).

rule.hazard: inability to audit input matches that led to system outputs
conditions: Annex III point 1(a) system
requirements: developer shall provide logging that records the input data for which the search has led to a match.

rule.hazard: deployers misinterpret outputs leading to inappropriate use
conditions: High-risk AI systems
requirements: Design and develop high-risk AI systems such that their operation is sufficiently transparent to enable deployers to interpret a system's output and use it appropriately.

rule.hazard: deployers cannot understand system operation due to missing or unclear instructions
conditions: High-risk AI systems
requirements: Accompany high-risk AI systems with instructions for use in an appropriate digital format or otherwise that include concise, complete, correct and clear information that is relevant, accessible and comprehensible to deployers.

rule.hazard: risks to health
conditions: instructions for use
requirements: provider shall include in the instructions for use any known or foreseeable circumstance, related to the use of the high-risk AI system, "which may lead to risks to the health".

rule.hazard: lower than expected accuracy, robustness or cybersecurity
conditions: instructions for use
requirements: provider shall include in the instructions for use the level of accuracy, including its metrics, robustness and cybersecurity referred to in Article 15 against which the high-risk AI system has been tested and validated and which can be expected, and any known and foreseeable circumstances that may have an impact on that expected level of accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity.

rule.hazard: unexpected performance limitations
conditions: instructions for use
requirements: provider shall include in the instructions for use the characteristics, capabilities and limitations of performance of the high-risk AI system.

rule.hazard: use outside intended purpose
conditions: instructions for use
requirements: provider shall include in the instructions for use the intended purpose of the high-risk AI system.

rule.hazard: inability to contact provider
conditions: instructions for use
requirements: provider shall include in the instructions for use the identity and the contact details of the provider and, where applicable, of its authorised representative.

rule.hazard: harm to health or safety
conditions: during the period in which they are in use
requirements: developer shall design and develop high-risk AI systems, including appropriate human-machine interface tools, so that natural persons can effectively oversee the system during the period in which it is in use.

rule.hazard: violation of fundamental rights
conditions: when used in accordance with its intended purpose
under conditions of reasonably foreseeable misuse
where such risks persist despite the application of other Section requirements
requirements: developer shall design human oversight measures to prevent or minimise risks to fundamental rights that may emerge from the system's intended use or from reasonably foreseeable misuse.

rule.hazard: insufficient human oversight due to mismatched measures
requirements: Provider shall ensure oversight measures are commensurate with the risks, level of autonomy and context of use of the high-risk AI system.

rule.hazard: lack of built-in oversight measures
conditions: when technically feasible
before it is placed on the market or put into service
requirements: Provider shall identify and build measures into the high-risk AI system when technically feasible before it is placed on the market or put into service.

rule.hazard: insufficient deployer-implementable oversight measures
conditions: before placing the high-risk AI system on the market or putting it into service
requirements: Provider shall identify, before placing the high-risk AI system on the market or putting it into service, measures that are appropriate to be implemented by the deployer.

rule.hazard: failure to detect or address anomalies, dysfunctions or unexpected performance
conditions: for the purpose of implementing paragraphs 1, 2 and 3
as appropriate and proportionate
requirements: Provider shall provide the high-risk AI system to the deployer in such a way that natural persons assigned human oversight are enabled to properly understand the system's relevant capacities and limitations and to duly monitor its operation, including detecting and addressing anomalies, dysfunctions and unexpected performance.

rule.hazard: automation bias leading to over-reliance on AI output
conditions: for the purpose of implementing paragraphs 1, 2 and 3
as appropriate and proportionate
requirements: Provider shall provide the high-risk AI system to the deployer in such a way that natural persons assigned human oversight are enabled to remain aware of the tendency to automatically rely or over-rely on the output produced by the high-risk AI system (automation bias).

rule.hazard: action based on unverified identification
conditions: Annex III 1(a) system
identification verified and confirmed by at least two natural persons with the necessary competence, training and authority
exceptions: high-risk AI systems used for law enforcement, migration, border control or asylum where Union or national law considers application disproportionate
requirements: deployer must not take any action or decision on the basis of the identification resulting from the system.

rule.hazard: insufficient accuracy
requirements: Developer shall design and develop high-risk AI systems to achieve an appropriate level of accuracy.

rule.hazard: insufficient robustness
requirements: Developer shall design and develop high-risk AI systems to achieve an appropriate level of robustness.

rule.hazard: insufficient resilience to errors, faults, or inconsistencies
requirements: Developer shall design and develop high-risk AI systems to be as resilient as possible to errors, faults or inconsistencies within the system or its operating environment, including interactions with natural persons or other systems.

rule.hazard: insufficient cybersecurity
requirements: Developer shall design and develop high-risk AI systems to achieve an appropriate level of cybersecurity.

rule.hazard: inconsistent performance across lifecycle
requirements: Developer shall design and develop high-risk AI systems so that they perform consistently in accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity throughout their lifecycle.

rule.hazard: undeclared accuracy metrics
requirements: Org shall declare the levels of accuracy and the relevant accuracy metrics of high-risk AI systems in the accompanying instructions of use.

rule.hazard: biased output feedback loop
conditions: systems that continue to learn after being placed on the market or put into service
requirements: developer shall develop high-risk AI systems that continue to learn after being placed on the market or put into service to eliminate or reduce as far as possible the risk of possibly biased outputs influencing input for future operations.

rule.hazard: unmitigated feedback loops
conditions: systems that continue to learn after being placed on the market or put into service
requirements: developer shall ensure that any feedback loops in high-risk AI systems that continue to learn after being placed on the market or put into service are duly addressed with appropriate mitigation measures.

rule.hazard: unauthorised alteration or exploitation of system
requirements: developer shall ensure high-risk AI systems are resilient against attempts by unauthorised third parties to alter their use, outputs or performance by exploiting system vulnerabilities.

rule.hazard: cybersecurity breach
conditions: for high-risk AI systems
requirements: org shall ensure that the technical solutions aiming to ensure the cybersecurity of high-risk AI systems are appropriate to the relevant circumstances and the risks.
